mnemonics: Decentralized, Distributed, Digital Ledger (The 3 D's of a blockchain)
Hashing Chains Blocks Together (HCBT - Helps recall the core components: Hash, Chain, Block, Transaction)
keyPoints: A blockchain is a decentralized and distributed digital ledger.
Data is stored in blocks that are cryptographically linked together in a chain.
The network is maintained by a peer-to-peer consensus mechanism, not a central authority.
commonMistakes: Thinking 'blockchain' and 'cryptocurrency' are the same thing (crypto is one application).
Believing that data on a blockchain is always private (data is often pseudonymous and transparent).
Assuming a blockchain is inherently faster than a central database (decentralized consensus often adds latency).
